subject: Public Health
title: Edward Jenner and the First Vaccine
description: A quick win: understand Edward Jenner and the First Vaccine.

hook.text: In 1796, a doctor infected a boy with cowpox pus—and accidentally invented vaccination.

buildup.text: Edward Jenner noticed milkmaids who caught cowpox never got smallpox—a deadly disease killing millions.

discovery.text: He inoculated 8-year-old James Phipps with cowpox, then exposed him to smallpox. The boy didn't get sick.

twist.text: The medical establishment initially mocked Jenner—satirical cartoons showed vaccinated people growing cow parts.

climax.text: Within decades, vaccination spread worldwide. The word 'vaccine' comes from 'vacca'—Latin for cow.

punchline.text: A milkmaid's immunity inspired the invention that saved more lives than any.

quiz.question: Where does the word 'vaccine' come from?
options: From the name of a scientist
From a Greek word meaning shield
